description_long: This sculpture depicts a nude male athlete in the dynamic pose of preparing to throw a discus, reminiscent of the classical Greek Discobolus. The figure is highly detailed, with well-defined musculature and a sense of motion captured in the twist of the torso and the extension of the arms. The left arm is raised and holds a round disc, while the right arm is lowered and bent, balancing the pose. The figure stands on a rectangular base, with one leg bent and the other extended behind, emphasizing the athletic tension. The surface is smooth and appears to be made of white marble or a similar stone, with subtle textural variations. The overall impression is one of strength, athleticism, and classical beauty.
description: A white marble-like sculpture of a nude male athlete in a discus-throwing pose, with detailed musculature and dynamic movement.
description_view_0: Front view showing the athlete's face, chest, and the discus held high in the left hand, with the right arm reaching downward.
description_view_1: Top-down rear view highlighting the back, shoulders, and the curvature of the spine, with the head and discus visible from above.
description_view_2: Rear three-quarter view emphasizing the back muscles, the bent right leg, and the extended left arm holding the discus.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the left side of the body, the outstretched arm with the discus, and the dynamic twist of the torso.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
